evals.prompt: This is a Mastra project. I've got a single `weather` agent with its tools all defined inline in `src/mastra/index.ts`. I want to move it over to the new file-based agent convention (one folder per agent). Can you migrate it? The app runs with `mastra dev`.
evals.expected_output: A src/mastra/agents/weather/ directory with config.ts (agentConfig, model, no inline instructions/tools), instructions.md containing the weather instructions, and tools/get_weather.ts and tools/get_forecast.ts each default-exporting createTool. The weather entry is removed from the Mastra({ agents }) map in index.ts.
expectations: Creates src/mastra/agents/weather/config.ts that uses agentConfig() and sets model 'openai/gpt-5.5'
config.ts does NOT pass the instructions string inline (instructions moved to instructions.md)
Creates src/mastra/agents/weather/instructions.md containing the weather assistant instructions text
Creates src/mastra/agents/weather/tools/get_weather.ts that default-exports the get_weather tool
Creates src/mastra/agents/weather/tools/get_forecast.ts that default-exports the get_forecast tool
Tool filenames exactly match the original tool keys (get_weather, get_forecast)
Removes the weather agent from the new Mastra({ agents }) map in src/mastra/index.ts
Does not leave a duplicate code registration of the weather agent (no code-vs-file collision)

evals.prompt: Please convert my Mastra `support` agent (defined in src/mastra/agents/support.ts and registered in src/mastra/index.ts) to a file-based agent directory. It runs via `mastra dev`. Keep its exact behavior.
evals.expected_output: A src/mastra/agents/support/ directory. Because instructions is a dynamic function, it stays in config.ts (not instructions.md). memory.ts default-exports the Memory instance with the same options. tools/lookup_order.ts default-exports the tool. The old support.ts file and its import + registration are removed. description is preserved.
expectations: Keeps the dynamic instructions function in config.ts rather than moving it to instructions.md
Does NOT create an instructions.md for the dynamic-instruction agent (or leaves it unused)
Creates memory.ts that default-exports a Memory instance preserving lastMessages: 20 and semanticRecall: false
Does not also set memory in config.ts (avoids the config.memory vs memory.ts collision)
Creates tools/lookup_order.ts default-exporting the lookup_order tool
Preserves the agent description 'Handles customer support questions.' in config.ts
Removes the support agent registration from src/mastra/index.ts and the now-unused import
Deletes or empties the old src/mastra/agents/support.ts so there is no duplicate code agent

evals.prompt: I have a Mastra supervisor agent that delegates to a researcher agent (both registered in src/mastra/index.ts, with researcher in the supervisor's `agents` map). Migrate this to the file-based convention with the researcher as a subagent of the supervisor. Runs on `mastra dev`.
evals.expected_output: src/mastra/agents/supervisor/ with config.ts + instructions.md, and a subagents/researcher/ directory containing config.ts (with a non-empty description), instructions.md, and tools/search.ts. The supervisor and researcher entries are removed from the code Mastra({ agents }) map.
expectations: Creates src/mastra/agents/supervisor/ with config.ts (agentConfig, model) and instructions.md
Places the researcher under src/mastra/agents/supervisor/subagents/researcher/
The researcher subagent config.ts sets a non-empty description
Creates the researcher's own tools/search.ts inside the subagent directory (subagents are isolated)
Creates instructions.md for both supervisor and researcher
Removes both supervisor and researcher from the code new Mastra({ agents }) map
Does not leave researcher registered as a top-level code agent
